title: adenostyles alpina subsp. pyrenaica
intro: Adenostyles alpina subsp. pyrenaica, also known as Pyrenean horseshoe bent or Pyrenean false hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Pyrenees mountains. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations. The health benefits of Adenostyles alpina subsp. pyrenaica include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
